paragraphs.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. My personal view on the European Union initiative on the Digital Fairness Act project. Frequent updates are currently required in the area of digital regulation, in this case consumers are in an even more vulnerable position. It is a subject where more and more companies take advantage of the acquisition of information through privacy policies or cookies that lead the consumer to make a bad practice of using the internet. Being more specific with the use of dark patterns, consumers are increasingly seen as meaning when they conclude a distance contract, feeling increasingly coerced or misled by the companies themselves. For all these reasons, the introduction of this new law is a necessity for the scope of consumer and user protection law. The future that awaits this type of practices that harm consumers will be progressing and the legislator will have a duty to continue to regulate these breaches. Not only for what we observe today, if not the protection of new technological generations, thereby shielding practices that we are currently suffering from such as the infinite scrolling, click-fatigue, covert advertising. As a student, I see the clear need for society, which requires more regulation to safeguard its rights as consumers.
